tate of Caliibrllia 
air Political Practices CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
107 SOUTH BROADWAY 
• •• 
SUITE 
7 00 7 
••• 
LOS ANGELES, 90012 
Hrs. Jennifer Wyatt 
Honorable Richard Katz 
15226 Parthenia, Suite 3-B 
Sepulveda, California 91343 
Dear Jennie: 
September 21, 1981 
A~/-/1/ 
This letter is to verify my advice to you during our telephone 
conversation of September 18, 1981. 
The facts, as you presented them are as follows: 
1. 
Assemblyman Richard Katz has a controlled committee. 
2. 
The controlled committee recently filed a semi-annual 
campaign statement for the period ending June 30, 1981. 
3. 
During the period of July 1st through September 17th, the 
Assemblyman's committee made a $15,000 contribution to the Assembly 
Democratic Fund, a recipient committee registered with the 
Secretary of State's office. 
The question you asked was does the $15,000 contribution to 
the Assembly Democratic Fund by the Assemblymen's Conrnittee require 
the filing of the next Threshold campaign statement? 
I advised that Government Code Section 84200(b) , in part, 
states that "candidates, ... their controlled committees ... that 
has made or received contributions of five hundred dollars ($500) 
or more ... during the threshold periods ... shall file campaign 
statements on ... (3) September 22, with a five hundred dollar 
($500) threshold period of July 1 through September 17." 
There-
fore, Assemblyman Katz and his controlled committee are required 
to file the third Threshold campaign state~ent since the 
contribution was over tqe $500 level of activity and it was made 
to another campaign committee during the threshold period. 
I hope this information has been helpful, please do not hesitate 
to call on me if I can be of any further.assistance. 
HPA: j g 
SACRAMENTO (916) 322·5660

sections.question: Does the $15,000 contribution to the Assembly Democratic Fund by the Assemblyman's Committee require the filing of the next Threshold campaign statement?
sections.conclusion: Assemblyman Katz and his controlled committee are required to file the third Threshold campaign statement since the contribution was over the $500 level of activity and it was made to another campaign committee during the threshold period.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a $15,000 contribution made by an Assemblyman's controlled committee to another campaign committee during the July 1 - September 17 threshold period requires filing a threshold campaign statement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, the filing is required because the $15,000 contribution exceeds the $500 threshold under Government Code Section 84200(b) and was made during the applicable threshold period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter with standard format; question and conclusion explicitly stated. Document verifies telephone advice given on September 18, 1981. Minor OCR artifacts in header ('Caliibrllia' for 'California', 'CommissioIL' for 'Commission') but body text is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the $15,000 contribution to the Assembly Democratic Fund by the Assemblyman's Committee require the filing of the next Threshold campaign statement?

CONCLUSION: Assemblyman Katz and his controlled committee are required to file the third Threshold campaign statement since the contribution was over the $500 level of activity and it was made to another campaign committee during the threshold period.
